sourceMovie.title: End of Days
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit End of Days for the same reason it broke through in 1999: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a horror rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Peter Hyams keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Constantine
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If End of Days clicked for you, Constantine (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Mummy Returns
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If End of Days clicked for you, The Mummy Returns (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and fantasy, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after End of Days?
faq.answer: Try Constantine, The Mummy Returns, Inland Empire—each shares DNA with End of Days while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is End of Days?
faq.answer: End of Days (1999) is best described as Action, Horror, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is End of Days worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
